channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As affordable housing disappears, states scramble to shore up the losses
item.description: While Americans continue to struggle under high rents, as many as 223,0000 affordable housing units could disappear in the next five years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedyan And Arushi Gupta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A year into the Israel-Hamas war, students say a chill on free speech has reached college classrooms
item.description: Campuses across the U.S. have been calmer in the wake of last spring’s protests, yet many are mired in a lingering unease.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Can someone find out who you voted for? No. Here is what you should know
item.description: As Election Day approaches, many Americans are questioning the secrecy of their ballots. We explain what you should know about privacy and casting a ballot.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Soros-linked dark money group props up Nebraska independent candidate in key Senate race
item.description: An independent candidate in Nebraska's Senate race has managed to make the election more competitive, with millions of outside money being poured into the state on his behalf.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden talks election, economy and Middle East in surprise news briefing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After 1,080 days as president, Joe Biden on Friday decided to pop in and take questions in the White House briefing room for the first time, striding in with a grin after a strong monthly jobs report and the temporary settlement of a strike by ports workers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China could wage economic war on Taiwan to force surrender, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. military officials and analysts have for years warned of possible armed attacks or blockades by China on Taiwan, but a report released Friday has raised a red flag about possible non-military tactics that could be used effectively against the self-governed island.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Dept. officials hinted at Israeli 'war crimes,' pushed Gaza aid days after Oct. 7 Hamas attack: report
item.description: Emails between senior officials show some high-ranking Biden administration figures believed Israel could be committing war crimes with the U.S. complicit from inaction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump national security advisers mock Biden's warnings to Israel to stick to ‘proportional’ Iran response
item.description: The Biden administration has now shifted its priority to containment, helping the region avoid all-out war between its two hegemonic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Gallego rakes in close to $22M in third quarter for Arizona Senate race
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) raised close to $22 million in the third quarter of fundraising for this year in the Arizona Senate race, his campaign announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: The ruling came one day before the temporary restraining order would expire, allowing the administration to continue the program while the case is moved to Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: In excerpt from new memoir, Melania Trump says women have the ‘right to choose’ abortion
item.description: The former first lady says she is a longtime supporter of abortion rights. Her memoir is coming out a year after former President Donald Trump said he was "able to kill Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC Schools Chancellor Banks to step down sooner than expected
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Schools Chancellor David Banks is expected to step down from his position sooner than expected, several months before he was supposed to retire, a City Hall spokesperson confirmed to PIX11 News on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Ranked choice voting is on the ballot in several states
item.description: Ranked choice voting could be coming to states like Colorado, Nevada and Oregon. Here's what to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge unseals new Trump Jan. 6 election interference evidence
item.description: Prosecutors provided the most detailed look yet at their election interference case against former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Meet the candidates running for San Diego City Attorney
item.description: There are only two candidates in the running to become San Diego City Attorney after incumbent Mara Elliott ends her last term in office in December.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: “This strike is about fairness. Foreign-owned shipping companies have made record profits and executive compensation has grown,” Harris said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Soomaalida St. Cloud waxay rabaan in ay ka dhex muuqdaan siyaasadda, laakiin iyaga oo dhan isku aragti ma aha
item.description: In ka badan labaatameeyo sano ka hor ayey qaxootigan oo ka soo jeeda Bariga Afrika soo dageen St. Cloud, oo qaarkood ayaa raadinaya in ay door ku yeeshaan maamulka iyo siyaasadda.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Walz’s account of son’s brush with gun violence offers poignant moment in VP debate
item.description: “Look, I’ve got a 17-year-old and he witnessed a shooting at a community center playing volleyball,” Walz said during the debate, referencing his son Gus. “Those things don’t leave you.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
